**Website: [https://emergentworks-test.web.app/designs/stories/](https://emergentworks-test.web.app/designs/stories/)**

**Home Page:**

- **Featured Story:**
  - Pull strong testimony from this [doc](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1lkaKVfUy85hnEL28de4L6yduArqDchnem8acjtFG5t4/edit?tab=t.o3rr768omhq7)
- **Our Programs:**
  - Title: Instead of Three Pathways. Thousand of Stories \- Make it= Two Pathways. Hundreds of Stories.
  - Technical Mentorship: Make it to TECK instead for anywhere where it says Technical Mentorship
- **Community Voices: In Their Own Words**
  - Can this section be a bit smaller as it feels too big visually?
  - Also can it be where you can click to the next story on the side, instead of listing the stories below? Other testimonials can be found [here](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1lkaKVfUy85hnEL28de4L6yduArqDchnem8acjtFG5t4/edit?tab=t.o3rr768omhq7)
  - There is a horizontal line between the image and text, that appears to be orange, can that line our branding green instead?

**About Us Page:**

- Replace the content on that page with this content and layout instead:
  - [https://emergentworks-test.web.app/designs/impact-first/about/](https://emergentworks-test.web.app/designs/impact-first/about/)
  - But with this text incorporated too: In 2020, Army Armstead Co-founded Emergent Works with a conviction born from lived experience: the people most affected by mass incarceration deserved more than a second chance — they deserved the tools to build entirely new futures. Having navigated the criminal justice system himself, Army understood that the gap between release and reentry wasn't just about skills. It was about dignity, community, and belief.What began as a small mentorship initiative in New York City has grown into a comprehensive organization offering three distinct programs that address the full spectrum of needs — from digital literacy and music production to career development and community engagement.
  - And if any of the text says 3 pathways, please change to 2 instead.
- **Our Journey:** This part has to be edited, we will add these updates with Army’s input on the biggest milestones.
- **Our Approach:** Make that section like this one from our chosen site: [https://emergentworks-test.web.app/designs/stories/about/](https://emergentworks-test.web.app/designs/stories/about/)

**Our Programs Page:**

- **Every Program Begins A Story:**
  - Under that it says Three Pathways, change to Two Pathways.
- **Before we get to the program breakdowns,** please add the below section just like it showed on the website draft:
  - How Our Programs Work: Our programs support system-impacted youth and adults in building the digital skills, confidence, and community needed to thrive in today’s world. Led by instructors and mentors who reflect our participants’ lived experiences, our programs blend creative expression, technical training, and social-emotional learning. We offer programming in person at our location as well as across New York City and virtual upon request, creating safe, inclusive, and culturally responsive spaces where participants can explore their interests, develop new skills, and prepare for meaningful careers. Every program is designed to restore dignity, build agency, and open doors to long-term opportunity.
- **After each section on T.RAP & TECK:**
  - It shows Read The Full Story, can you also add a button next to it that says Apply. And let’s remove dates, let it just say July \- June. And let’s make this alterations to the page that pops up once you click Read Full Story:
    - For T.RAP: Make it into this [layout](https://emergentworks-test.web.app/designs/impact-first/programs/trap/) instead with all the information, but with some alterations on the info:
      - Weekly Schedule: Mondays: Digital Literacy and Workreadiness (5 hours), Tuesdays: Therapeutic Song Writing and Workreadiness (5 hours), Wednesdays: Studio Recording and Workreadiness and Thursdays (5 hours): Certificate Training and Guest Speakers (5 hours)
      - Journey: let’s not make them timebound, but more so like these are the various elements for the program:
        - ## **01**

        - **Community Research & Participatory Action**  
           Development of a community research project using a Participatory Action Research (PAR) methodology. Participants identify key issues impacting their communities, conduct research, gather and analyze data, and transform findings into informed insights that drive creative and social outcomes.
        - ***

        - ## **02**

        - **Album Creation & Artistic Development**  
           Development of a collaborative album featuring both solo and group songs rooted in the themes uncovered through research. Focus includes artistry development, songwriting, recording, performance skills, and message alignment.
        - ***

        - ## **03**

        - **Digital & Career Development Projects**  
           Creation of multiple professional digital assets including a personal website, resume, cover letter, LinkedIn profile, job tracker, and career dashboard. Participants build essential digital competencies such as Google Suite proficiency, website building, Canva design, and foundational project management skills.
        - ***

        - ## **04**

        - **Social-Emotional Learning & Mental Wellness**  
           Ongoing social-emotional development through music and arts-based workshops, complemented by dedicated mental health sessions with a licensed social worker to support reflection, resilience, and personal growth.
        - ***

        - ## **05**

        - **Workforce & Education Pathways**  
           Connection to job opportunities, certification programs, and educational pathways. Participants receive application support, interview preparation, and workforce readiness training to strengthen long-term employment outcomes.
        - ***

        - ## **06**

        - **Culminating Showcase & Public Presentation**  
           Preparation for a final showcase event featuring live performances of recorded album tracks and presentation of digital projects. Participants gain public exposure, confidence, and experience presenting their work to an audience of peers, partners, and community stakeholders.

      - Tools You’ll Learn: Pro Tools, Bandcamp, Google Suite, Chat GPT/ Gemini, Canva.

    - For TECK:
      - Make it into this [layout](https://emergentworks-test.web.app/designs/impact-first/programs/trap/) instead with all the information, but with some alterations on the info:
        - Program Overview: Change to “More Than Technology” and then add below: This free 12-week virtual mentorship program supports system-impacted individuals (18+) across the United States by equipping them with the digital tools and skills needed to thrive in today’s workforce and beyond. Participants are paired one-on-one with industry professionals who provide personalized guidance in digital and AI literacy, coding basics, UI/UX, and practical applications of technology. Through weekly mentorship sessions and hands-on, portfolio-ready projects, participants build confidence, problem-solving skills, and real-world digital competencies. More than just learning technology, participants leave the program prepared to navigate professional environments, manage digital projects, create professional materials, and leverage digital tools for employment, education, and personal growth.
        - Instead of the categories right below that shows the music competencies, replace them with: Digital Literacy Basics, UI/UX Design, Coding Fundamentals, Project Development, Website Basics, Mentorship
        - Then for schedule: Coordinate weekly virtual sessions with your mentor, ensuring a minimum of two hours of engagement each week.
        - For Journey: **01**
        - **Mentor Matching & Goal Setting**  
           Participants are paired with an industry professional who provides one-on-one guidance. Together, they establish interest areas and goals for the mentorship, such as coding fundamentals, digital literacy, application support, or UI/UX design, creating a personalized learning roadmap.
        - ***

        - ## **02**

        - **Weekly Mentorship Sessions**  
           Participants meet virtually with their mentor **once a week for a minimum of two hours**, engaging in hands-on instruction, collaborative problem-solving, and career-focused guidance. These sessions build both technical skills and professional confidence.
        - ***

        - ## **03**

        - **Project-Based Learning**  
           Through mentorship, participants complete portfolio-ready projects that reflect their skills in digital & AI literacy, coding, UI/UX, and applied tech tools. Projects are designed to reinforce learning, demonstrate mastery, and create tangible outcomes for future employment or educational opportunities.
        - ***

        - ## **04**

        - **Digital & AI Skills Development**  
           Participants strengthen essential digital competencies, including Google Suite, Canva, website building, and foundational project management. These skills equip participants to navigate modern professional environments and manage digital workflows effectively.
        - ***

        - ## **05**

        - **Mentor Relationship & Professional Growth**  
           Ongoing one-on-one mentorship fosters a strong professional relationship, providing guidance, feedback, and insight into digital industry practices. Participants gain soft skills such as problem-solving, communication, and workplace readiness, building confidence for future opportunities.
        - ***

        - ## **06**

        - **Portfolio Completion**  
           At the conclusion of the program, participants compile their completed projects into a professional portfolio. This includes digital assets, work samples, and applied skills, providing a showcase of their growth and readiness for employment, education, or further technical training.

      - Potential Tools You’ll Learn: Google Suite, Chat GPT/ Gemini, Canva, Figma, The Odin Project, Website Building Softwares.

- **Partner Programs:** Bringing Stories to our Communities:
  - Add this right after the above sub-title just like on the website draft: Non-profit organizations can book our T.RAP and TECK programs as well as customized programs to serve their communities. T.RAP centers social-emotional learning, therapeutic writing, and music production, while TECK builds digital literacy skills including Google Suite, AI, Canva, website development, and entrepreneurial foundations. We tailor each cohort to the specific needs of our partners — blending creative expression, workforce readiness, digital skills, and entrepreneurship as well as other themes requested to equip participants with hands-on learning, mentorship, and real-world opportunities. Empower your participants with hands-on learning, mentorship, and real-world opportunities.
- **T.RAP and TECK box:**
  - They are shortened down, they need to show what it showed on the draft website.
  - Also they need a button that says something about booking or reaching out to us to discuss their needs etc.
  - Make them more like this image below in terms of text, not color wise as let’s keep the dark green, but more so how it’s laid out with full information & softwares/skills:
    - Text modification for TECK:
      - Our TECK program offers flexible digital literacy training exclusively for partner organizations through a fee-for-service model that also integrates alumni workforce development. Cohorts run 4, 8, or 12 weeks and provide participants with hands-on experience in Google Suite, AI tools, Canva, and basic website building. In addition to being led by Emergent Works’ proven curriculum and instructors, alumni are trained and engaged as teaching assistants and cohort facilitators—creating paid leadership opportunities that expand our impact beyond a single cohort. TECK not only equips communities with essential technical skills, confidence, and workforce readiness, but also reinvests in alumni by developing them as educators and industry-ready professionals.
    - Text modification for T.RAP:
      - Our T.RAP program offers immersive social-emotional learning and creative skills training exclusively for partner organizations through a fee-for-service model that also supports alumni workforce development. Cohorts run 8–12+ weeks, guiding participants through therapeutic writing, music production, and leadership development. In addition to being led by Emergent Works’ experienced instructors, alumni are intentionally trained and engaged as mentors and teaching facilitators when partner organizations book a cohort—creating paid leadership opportunities that expand our impact. T.RAP not only empowers communities with emotional resilience and creative expression, but also empowers alumni by developing them as educators, leaders, and creative professionals.

    ![][image1]

- **Ready To Begin?**
  - This section AI made for “if you wanna volunteer, donate, mentor etc”. Please replace that with the success story at the bottom of the page of the Programs page on the [draft website](https://tine945.wixsite.com/emergent-works-1/programs). And let’s have an impact page button just like on the [draft website](https://tine945.wixsite.com/emergent-works-1/programs). Which also means we would need an impact page on the new website as well that shows the content from the [draft website](https://tine945.wixsite.com/emergent-works-1/programs), and also has our digital projects and annual reports etc.

**Our Team Page:**

- **Replace Angie’s blurb with this:** Angie manages all communication, social media, and design for Emergent Works, ensuring weekly marketing that highlights the organization’s programs, participant achievements, and overall impact. Her creativity and dedication help showcase the important work EW does in the community.

**Get Involved Page:**

- **Mentor Testimony:**
  - We can pull testimonies from this [doc](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1lkaKVfUy85hnEL28de4L6yduArqDchnem8acjtFG5t4/edit?usp=sharing)
- **Donation Amounts:**
  - $100 \- $100 covers weekly stipend for one T.RAP student to learn digital literacy, SEL, music production and partake in certificate trainings.
  - $250 \- $250 covers one week of paid hours for an alumni instructor to gain more work experience.
  - $1000 \- $1000 covers graduation stipends for 10 T.RAP graduates obtaining essential digital, music production and workforce readiness skills and completing milestone projects.
    - Or $1000 covers 1 week of programming that includes 20 hours of digital literacy workshops, music therapy and studio recording sessions, 1-on-1 mentorship, guest speakers on essential topics, and work readiness support to obtain employment and certificates.
- **Get Involved:**
  - Under Mentor, second line: Change to “Min 2 hours a week transforms lives.”
  - Under Volunteer, Change to: Support events, outreach, and community building or join our Advisory Board. There's a place for every skill.
